title: Women's political empowerment index vs. electoral democracy index
subtitle: The indices are by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem), based on expert estimates, and range from 0 to 1 (most empowered/most democratic).
caption:
Women's political empowerment index vs. electoral democracy index

The indices are by V-Dem, based on expert estimates, and range from 0 to 1 (most empowered/most democratic).

This scatter plot displays the relationship between the Women's Political Empowerment Index on the vertical axis and the Electoral Democracy Index on the horizontal axis for various countries. Both indices range from 0 to 1, with higher values indicating greater empowerment or democracy. The data points are color-coded by region: North America (orange), South America (brown), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (light blue). A cluster of European countries, including Albania, Ukraine, Bulgaria, Croatia, and Malta, occupies the upper-right quadrant, with Electoral Democracy Index values generally above 0.6 and Women's Political Empowerment Index values ranging from approximately 0.8 to nearly 1.0. Australia and several South American nations like Brazil, Argentina, and Colombia also appear in this high-scoring region. In contrast, countries such as Afghanistan, Eritrea, and Sudan are positioned in the bottom-left corner, with both index values falling below 0.2. Asian countries show a wide distribution; Qatar and Iran sit low on the empowerment axis despite varying democracy scores, while Sri Lanka and Bhutan appear in the middle range. African nations like Ethiopia, Algeria, and Cote d'Ivoire cluster in the middle-left area, with empowerment scores between 0.6 and 0.8 but lower democracy scores around 0.3 to 0.4. The visual arrangement suggests a general trend where higher electoral democracy scores often coincide with higher women's political empowerment scores, though significant variation exists within regions.
